blocks.content: The closer Yahweh2 got, the more Chaos5MinutesBefore's expression twisted.

blocks.speaker: Chaos5MinutesBefore
blocks.content: What is this? What's going on?
He was used to bullying beginners in the neutral zone and had his own manual for it.
Most beginners had strong pride, so after a few provocations, they would charge in like Yahweh2.
Even if they attacked first, the moment they retaliated, both parties would become hostile according to High Gear's system.
In that state, if he eliminated the opponent, he could loot them, even if they were from a different faction.
The loot was random, either 10% of their galaxies or a specific part.
For a high-level player with trillions, 10% of galaxies would be a jackpot, but Chaos5MinutesBefore enjoyed getting parts from beginners.

blocks.speaker: Chaos5MinutesBefore
blocks.content: But…
Even with High Gear's output at maximum, Yahweh2 didn't get hit once.

blocks.speaker: Chaos5MinutesBefore
blocks.content: How is this happening?
Chaos5MinutesBefore was bewildered by the unexpected situation, and his evasion probability finally dropped below 20%.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You bastard!
Shirone shouted, raising his arm, and energy bullets pounded Chaos5MinutesBefore's body.

blocks.speaker: Chaos5MinutesBefore
blocks.content: Ugh!
Of course, due to his high durability, a few hits wouldn't kill him immediately.

blocks.speaker: Chaos5MinutesBefore
blocks.content: This is strange. It's a bug. A system error.
Chaos5MinutesBefore felt a chill.
It was like a cuckoo clock popping out at the wrong time, something too familiar to notice until it went wrong.

blocks.speaker: Chaos5MinutesBefore
blocks.content: As the distance closes, evasion probability drops. But the same goes for him.
The battle continued.
Shirone dodged through the barrage, landing several hits on Chaos5MinutesBefore.
Sparks flew from his body, and the durability of the damaged parts appeared in augmented reality.

blocks.speaker: Chaos5MinutesBefore
blocks.content: Heh. Heh heh.
Durability 482/500.
Though momentarily flustered by the abnormal situation, the numerical difference in power was clear.

blocks.speaker: Chaos5MinutesBefore
blocks.content: His weapon lacks power. In close combat, I'm much more advantageous.
And one more thing.

blocks.speaker: Chaos5MinutesBefore
blocks.content: I have this.
Activating the level 20 selective option, 'Turbo,' his output surged.

blocks.speaker: Chaos5MinutesBefore
blocks.content: Die!
Using energy bullets, which were affected by output, his firepower more than doubled.

blocks.speaker: Chaos5MinutesBefore
blocks.content: Ugh!
As the barrage speed increased, Shirone wrinkled his nose and heightened his concentration.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Time began to slow…
A cluster of round flashes, like dandelions, became clear.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: There.
It was geometry unfolding in space-time, and Shirone quickly found and exploited the gap.

blocks.speaker: Chaos5MinutesBefore
blocks.content: Chaos5MinutesBefore's heart sank.

blocks.speaker: Chaos5MinutesBefore
blocks.content: Ahhh!
Unable to endure the anxiety, he sprayed bullets wildly, but it only made things easier for Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Chaos5MinutesBefore
blocks.content: Alright! Let's see who wins!
Bang!
Chaos5MinutesBefore's eyes shook, and his companions' expressions turned blank.
Instead of continuing the firefight, Yahweh2 had punched Chaos5MinutesBefore in the face with his other hand.

blocks.speaker: Chaos5MinutesBefore
blocks.content: Ugh!
The unexpected blow knocked Chaos5MinutesBefore down, and Shirone pinned him to the ground.
With the gun barrel aimed at his head, Shirone saw the fear in the man's eyes.
Silence fell.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Why isn't he shooting?

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Maybe it's his first time killing someone? He seems sensitive about that.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Why did you attack us?

blocks.speaker: Chaos5MinutesBefore
blocks.content: What nonsense? Ugh!
Chaos5MinutesBefore tried to sit up, but Shirone pressed him down with his foot.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Apologize to Death Princess. Hurting others for no reason is wrong.

blocks.speaker: Chaos5MinutesBefore
blocks.content: What's this now?

blocks.speaker: Chaos5MinutesBefore
blocks.content: Is this a concept? No, I can still win. My durability is much higher.

blocks.speaker: Chaos5MinutesBefore
blocks.content: Alright, I'll apologize. Just let me go. I won't bother you again.

blocks.speaker: Death Princess
blocks.content: Don't believe him! He's lying!
As Shirone remained still, Chaos5MinutesBefore aimed his gun upwards.

blocks.speaker: Chaos5MinutesBefore
blocks.content: Of course, you fool.

blocks.speaker: Chaos5MinutesBefore
blocks.content: What?
Penetrate.
He was already a dead man.

blocks.speaker: Chaos5MinutesBefore
blocks.content: Wait, wait…!
Bang!
Shirone's shoulder twitched, and the energy bullet shattered Chaos5MinutesBefore's head.

blocks.speaker: Chaos5MinutesBefore
blocks.content: Gah… gah!
The headshot made the machine convulse and then stop with a mechanical sound.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Hey, Yahweh2.

blocks.speaker: Chaos5MinutesBefore
blocks.content: What's with this atmosphere?

blocks.speaker: Strongest Codename
blocks.content: Chaos5MinutesBefore's durability was around 500. It would take a long time for Shirone's weapon to penetrate. He must have calculated that.

blocks.speaker: Strongest Codename
blocks.content: He didn't seem to care about the person, just the calculation.

blocks.speaker: Death Princess
blocks.content: Probably. But…

blocks.speaker: Death Princess
blocks.content: He looks so sad.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Who was he?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What kind of life did he live in reality to feel sadness even in a simulation?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: We won. Is everyone okay?
As the heavy atmosphere lifted, Destroyer707 and his group finally moved.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: That was amazing. Dodging even energy bullets. Are you a combat genius?

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: I was lucky. If Chaos5MinutesBefore had been smarter, it would have been a tough fight.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: What are you talking about? It was a perfect victory.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If it were me, I would have retreated while fighting. With the old joints, you can keep an eye on the back while sprinting forward. It takes advantage of high output.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He probably didn't think that far in the heat of the moment. That's why I managed to take him down.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: It was a difference in judgment.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Underestimating the enemy, thinking there's no way I could lose—these things greatly affect the outcome of a battle.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: In the end, he was just a high-level opponent, not a particularly strong one.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: The AI system allows even combat novices to enjoy all the content.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: That's its big advantage, but…

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: That alone doesn't make a ranker. Rankers are those who understand and thoroughly exploit the system, not just rely on it.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Dodging energy bullets was a secondary issue compared to that.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: He's learning incredibly fast. Even under the same conditions, we'd lose now.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: We didn't feel particularly bad about it.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Well, he is a friend of Yolga's Son.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: There's something here?

blocks.speaker: Death Princess
blocks.content: Loot!

blocks.speaker: Death Princess
blocks.content: If you die while hostile, you can get loot. No one else can see it until you pick it up.

blocks.speaker: Death Princess
blocks.content: What is it? What dropped?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: This? It's the right arm that guy was using. Maron, Bolt Gun-Z204 model.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Haha! He dropped his most expensive part. Serves him right. He's probably fuming right now.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Can't it be restored? What about Death Princess's left arm?

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: No. Loot or parts with zero durability are permanently lost upon death.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: It's okay. I can buy my left arm as a single part from the shop.

blocks.speaker: Death Princess
blocks.content: Hmm, so what should we do with this? Death Princess can't use it since it's a right arm. Should we sell it for galaxies?

blocks.speaker: Strongest Codename
blocks.content: That's possible, but not recommended. The shop charges high fees.

blocks.speaker: Strongest Codename
blocks.content: And it's not a popular model, so selling it to users isn't ideal.

blocks.speaker: Strongest Codename
blocks.content: Mass-produced parts usable at level 20 have their limits.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Just use it yourself. That's the best option.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Let's find an engineer in Atogram. We need to replenish ammo anyway.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Okay. But an engineer?

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Shop items can be replaced immediately, but user-developed parts or loot need an engineer to install.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Large guilds even hire dedicated engineers.

blocks.speaker: Strongest Codename
blocks.content: So that's what you meant by 'you'll experience it eventually.'

blocks.speaker: Strongest Codename
blocks.content: Exactly. Shop parts are good, but legendary or artisan items are obtained through hunting or duels.

blocks.speaker: Strongest Codename
blocks.content: Don't worry too much. A right arm isn't that weird.

blocks.speaker: Strongest Codename
blocks.content: Personally, eye replacements are the most annoying.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Agreed.
Back in Atogram, they bought Death Princess's left arm and energy bullets from the Maron shop.
Next, they looked for an engineer.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Shops have engineers, but they're expensive. It's better to hire a user.

blocks.speaker: Destroyer707
blocks.content: Yeah, let's save money.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Even if we weren't short on galaxies, Shirone preferred to learn the basics.
Entering the shop of 'Hermes,' a famous engineer in Atogram, they saw a familiar face.
Huh?
